overall_justification: This edition of Correio da Manhã takes a strongly Orthodox stance against the proposed increase in the tax on "xarque" (dried meat).  The dominant narrative, presented through multiple articles and emphasized by placement and framing, centers on the negative impact on the poor and working class. The paper explicitly criticizes the Câmara (Chamber of Deputies) for prioritizing the interests of a small group of "xarque" producers from Rio Grande do Sul over the needs of the Brazilian people.  The arguments presented align perfectly with the Orthodox concern for the "probidade do paiz" (probity of the country) and the well-being of the general population, implicitly suggesting that the tax increase would be a harmful intervention in the market and detrimental to the nation's economic health.  While not directly mentioning the Caixa de Conversão, the focus on fiscal responsibility and the negative consequences of protectionist measures strongly suggest an Orthodox perspective on monetary policy as well.
